title: Ensure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service worker nodes are  updated to the latest image to ensure patching of vulnerabilities

input_row.title: Ensure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service worker nodes are  updated to the latest image to ensure patching of vulnerabilities
input_row.description: Update the worker nodes in a cluster to the latest patch version so that security fixes are applied to those worker nodes.
input_row.rationale: As security updates and patches are put in place for the API server and other master components, you must be sure that the worker nodes remain in sync. You can make two types of updates: updating only the patch version, or updating the major.minor version with the patch version. This benchmark is only in relation to updating the patch version, not the major or minor versions. Updating the patch version in a timely manner ensures patching of known vulnerabilities and will keep your worker nodes more secure.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Log in to the IBM Cloud console. 2. To view a list of your resources, go to Menu > Resource List. 3. From your IBM Cloud resource list, select your cluster. 4. Select the Worker Nodes tab. 5. For each listed worker node, ensure that next to the Version no information icon is present indicating that an update is available. If there is an information icon indicating there is an update available, then the worker node is not running the latest available image. From Command Line: 1. List the clusters in your IBM Cloud account ibmcloud ks cluster ls 2. List the workers for a given cluster. ibmcloud ks workers -c 3. Ensure that next to the version for a worker node no asterisk (*) is present indicating that an update is available. a. Here is an example output that shows that there is an available update for a worker node ibmcloud ks workers -c mykubecluster-free OK ID Public IP Private IP Flavor State Status Zone Version kube-btdsgk5d0t1lpluh7f40-mykubeclust-default-000000d1 173.193.100.98 10.76.216.200 free normal Ready hou02 1.17.11_1538* * To update to 1.17.12_1541 version, run 'ibmcloud ks worker update'. Review and make any required version changes before you update: 'https://ibm.biz/upworker' b. Here is an example output showing there are no available updates for a worker node ibmcloud ks workers -c mykubecluster-free OK ID Public IP Private IP Flavor State Status Zone Version kube-btdsgk5d0t1lpluh7f40-mykubeclust-default-000000d1 173.193.100.98 10.76.216.200 free normal Ready hou02 1.17.12_1541
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Log in to the IBM Cloud console. 2. Optional: Add capacity to your cluster by resizing the worker pool. The pods on the worker node can be rescheduled and continue running on the added worker nodes during the update. 3. To view a list of your resources, go to Menu > Resource List. 4. From your IBM Cloud resource list, select your cluster. 5. Select the Worker Nodes tab. 6. Select the checkbox for each worker node that you want to update. An action bar is displayed over the table header row. 7. From the action bar, click Update. From Command Line: 1. Complete the prerequisite steps. 2. Optional: Add capacity to your cluster by resizing the worker pool. The pods on the worker node can be rescheduled and continue running on the added worker nodes during the update. 3. List the worker nodes in your cluster and note the ID and Primary IP of the worker node that you want to update. ibmcloud ks worker ls --cluster 4. Replace the worker node to update the worker node to the latest patch version at the same major.minor version. ibmcloud ks worker replace --cluster --worker 5. Repeat these steps for each worker node that you want to update. 6. Optional: After the replaced worker nodes are in a Ready status, resize the worker pool to meet the cluster capacity that you want.
input_row.impact: The worker node update also updates the worker node to the same major.minor version as the master if a major.minor update is also available.
input_row.default_value: Worker nodes are not updated unless the user initiates an update.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**  
- The control can be fully verified via IBM Cloud CLI commands and APIs as outlined in the audit section.  
- Steps for listing the worker nodes and checking for updates are provided both in the audit and remediation sections, confirming that this is a standard service property exposed via CLI. 

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_kubernetes_worker_nodes_patch_version_updated

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**  
The automated program would perform the following checks:
- Use the command `ibmcloud ks cluster ls` to list all clusters and their IDs.
- For each cluster, execute `ibmcloud ks workers -c <cluster_id>` to retrieve the details of worker nodes.
- Validate the `Version` field for each listed worker node. Check that no asterisk (*) is present next to the version number to confirm that the worker node is running the latest patch version.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
    - Pass: Worker node version does not have an asterisk (*), indicating it is up to date.
    - Fail: Worker node version has an asterisk (*), indicating an available update.
- This automation will ensure compliance with the control that mandates that worker nodes must be updated to the latest image to patch vulnerabilities.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The task of verifying whether IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service worker nodes are updated to the latest image can be effectively automated using IBM Cloud CLI commands. The use of `ibmcloud ks cluster ls` and `ibmcloud ks workers -c <cluster_id>` is appropriate for retrieving the necessary information to determine compliance with the control.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The IBM Cloud CLI commands mentioned (`ibmcloud ks cluster ls` and `ibmcloud ks workers -c <cluster_id>`) are valid and can be used to list clusters and retrieve worker node details.
- The method of checking for an asterisk (*) next to the version number is a valid approach to determine if updates are available.
- The program name `ibm_kubernetes_worker_nodes_patch_version_updated` is somewhat descriptive but could be improved for clarity and adherence to naming conventions.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be more descriptive to clearly indicate the specific security check being performed. A better name might be `ibm_kubernetes_worker_nodes_latest_patch_check`.
- Consider adding a step to automatically trigger an update for non-compliant worker nodes, if feasible, to enhance the automation process.
- It might be beneficial to include a check for the specific version number against a known latest version list to ensure that the nodes are not only free of an asterisk but also running the latest version.
- Ensure that the CLI environment is set up to handle potential errors or exceptions, such as network issues or authentication failures.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded, and the use of IBM Cloud CLI for this compliance check is appropriate. The technical details provided align with IBM Cloud capabilities, and the approach is consistent with security best practices for ensuring that worker nodes are updated.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `ibm_kubernetes_worker_nodes_latest_patch_check` for better clarity and adherence to naming conventions.
- Consider adding additional validation steps to compare the version numbers against a known latest version list for more robust compliance verification.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
Both AI opinions agree that the task of verifying whether IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service worker nodes are updated to the latest image can be automated using IBM Cloud CLI commands. The IBM Cloud CLI provides the necessary commands to list clusters and check the version of worker nodes, making it feasible to automate this compliance check. The method of checking for an asterisk (*) next to the version number is a valid approach to determine if updates are available, which aligns with the control's requirements.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
ibm_kubernetes_worker_nodes_latest_patch_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- IBM Cloud Resource/Service: IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service
- API/CLI Method: `ibmcloud ks cluster ls` and `ibmcloud ks workers -c <cluster_id>`
- Properties to Check: Version field of each worker node
- Pass Criteria: Worker node version does not have an asterisk (*), indicating it is up to date.
- Fail Criteria: Worker node version has an asterisk (*), indicating an available update.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**
There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported the automated approach. The second opinion suggested a more descriptive program name, which has been adopted for clarity.
